ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (q:Question {title: 'How to build a Cypher query?'})-[r1:TAGGED]->(tag:Tag {name: 'programming'}),
    p2 = (u:User {uuid: 123})-[r2:ASKED]->(q),
    p3 = (u)-[r3:PROVIDED]->(a:Answer {title: 'Correct approach'})-[r4:ANSWERED]->(q),
    p4 = (a)-[r5:COMMENTED_ON]->(q)-[:TAGGED]->(tag)-[:COMMENTED]->(c:Comment {score: 15})
RETURN p1, p2, p3, p4
ground.question: How has the programming tag influenced the acceptance rate of the answer titled "Correct approach" in the question about building a Cypher query, and what role did the 15-point-scoring comment play in its community reception?

cypher: MATCH (q:Question {title: "Building a Cypher query"})<-[:COMMENTED_ON]-(c:Comment {score: 15})\nMATCH (a:Answer {title: "Correct approach"})<-[:ANSWERED]-(q)<-[:TAGGED]-(t:Tag)\nRETURN q.acceptance_rate, t.name
